buildBoundary.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks corroborate safe-to-store visual evidence.
buildBoundary.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
buildBoundary.label: Build & Test destructive boundary appears before activation
buildBoundary.limitation: Without explicit disposable build-confirm mode, the runner still cancels at the boundary.
buildBoundary.proof: Build & Test opens a confirmation dialog before activation; disposable runs confirm it and wait for Step 3.

console.accessibilityRiskReason: The claim depends on accessibility text without a screenshot, usually due sensitive-surface redaction.
console.evidenceStrengthReason: Pass relies on redacted accessibility text only, usually because screenshots are intentionally suppressed for a sensitive surface.
console.label: Console opens and renders
console.limitation: Does not prove log completeness and may omit secret-bearing visuals by design.
console.proof: Sensitive screenshot omitted; redacted accessibility text must show Console/log content.

customizationSaveRollback.accessibilityRiskReason: The claim has non-UI state or manifest proof in addition to UI evidence.
customizationSaveRollback.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use path is backed by independent disposable git state proof.
customizationSaveRollback.label: Untracked macOS customizations can be saved and rolled back
customizationSaveRollback.limitation: If no untracked customizations chip is visible, the scenario records a no-op pass because there is nothing to save.
customizationSaveRollback.proof: When the untracked customizations chip is visible, Computer Use adds it to config, confirms Build & Test, commits Step 3, then restores the disposable baseline and verifies the remote git tree is clean.

diff.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks corroborate safe-to-store visual evidence.
diff.evidenceStrengthReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks provide binding visual corroboration where screenshots are safe to store.
diff.label: Diff tab renders after intent review
diff.limitation: Does not prove the change builds or saves; save/rollback scenarios provide that remote git proof.
diff.proof: Diff view must expose the candidate Homebrew config file or requested package/change.

discard.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source without binding screenshot checks.
discard.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
discard.label: Discard confirmation is guarded and only confirmed in disposable state
discard.limitation: When History restore cleanup passes, Discard itself is intentionally not clicked because the disposable config is already back at baseline.
discard.proof: Discard opens a confirmation boundary when used. In full-lifecycle runs, the stronger History restore cleanup path can supersede Discard and is proven by rollback artifacts.

feedback.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks corroborate safe-to-store visual evidence.
feedback.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
feedback.label: Give Feedback opens and can be cancelled
feedback.proof: Computer Use opened and cancelled the feedback dialog.

history.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks corroborate safe-to-store visual evidence.
history.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
history.label: My History opens and renders
history.limitation: Current run does not prove a newly saved change appears there.
history.proof: Computer Use opened History and captured a visible history/empty state.

homebrewSaveRollback.accessibilityRiskReason: The claim has non-UI state or manifest proof in addition to UI evidence.
homebrewSaveRollback.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use path is backed by independent disposable git state proof.
homebrewSaveRollback.label: Untracked Homebrew items can be saved and rolled back
homebrewSaveRollback.limitation: If no untracked Homebrew chip is visible, the scenario records a no-op pass because there is nothing to save.
homebrewSaveRollback.proof: When the untracked Homebrew chip is visible, Computer Use adds it to config, confirms Build & Test, commits Step 3, then restores the disposable baseline and verifies the remote git tree is clean.

launch.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks corroborate safe-to-store visual evidence.
launch.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
launch.label: App launches and first screen is usable
launch.limitation: Does not prove provider or config state.
launch.proof: Accessibility text shows the nixmac window, Settings/History/Feedback controls, stepper, prompt text area, and disabled Send button.

mainCoverageFreshness.label: Main branch user-visible coverage stays mapped

prSpecificCoverage.label: PR-specific user-visible behavior is covered when applicable

reportInspection.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks corroborate safe-to-store visual evidence.
reportInspection.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
reportInspection.label: Generated HTML report is inspected with Computer Use
reportInspection.limitation: Does not prove a human reviewed every screenshot.
reportInspection.proof: Computer Use opens the generated report on the remote Mac and sees report sections.

reportIssue.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks corroborate safe-to-store visual evidence.
reportIssue.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
reportIssue.label: Report Issue opens and can be cancelled
reportIssue.proof: Computer Use opened and cancelled the report issue dialog.

review.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks corroborate safe-to-store visual evidence.
review.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
review.label: Real provider workflow reaches Review
review.proof: Polling reached Review-equivalent UI with Build & Test/Discard/Summary/Diff controls.

rollbackCleanup.accessibilityRiskReason: The claim has non-UI state or manifest proof in addition to UI evidence.
rollbackCleanup.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use path is backed by independent disposable git state proof.
rollbackCleanup.label: Rollback cleanup returns disposable config to clean state
rollbackCleanup.limitation: Only runs when Save succeeded and a restorable disposable baseline exists.
rollbackCleanup.proof: After Save, Computer Use opens History, restores the pre-test baseline commit, and the runner verifies HEAD returned to that baseline with a clean worktree.

saveFlow.accessibilityRiskReason: The claim has non-UI state or manifest proof in addition to UI evidence.
saveFlow.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use path is backed by independent disposable git state proof.
saveFlow.label: Step 3 Save / Keep changes persists a change
saveFlow.limitation: When disposable build-confirm mode is not enabled, Save remains untested.
saveFlow.proof: In disposable build-confirm mode, Computer Use reaches Step 3, clicks Commit, and the runner verifies the disposable repo HEAD changed with a clean worktree.

settingsAIModels.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks corroborate safe-to-store visual evidence.
settingsAIModels.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
settingsAIModels.label: Settings AI Models tab visibly renders
settingsAIModels.limitation: Does not change models or verify every provider.
settingsAIModels.proof: Computer Use opened AI Models and captured provider/model/build controls.

settingsAPIKeys.accessibilityRiskReason: The claim depends on accessibility text without a screenshot, usually due sensitive-surface redaction.
settingsAPIKeys.evidenceStrengthReason: Pass relies on redacted accessibility text only, usually because screenshots are intentionally suppressed for a sensitive surface.
settingsAPIKeys.label: Settings API Keys tab visibly renders
settingsAPIKeys.limitation: Does not edit/delete keys and does not prove keychain persistence by itself.
settingsAPIKeys.proof: API Keys screenshot is captured only when raw accessibility text confirms no unmasked key-like secret is present; redacted text must show API Keys/OpenRouter/API-key controls.

settingsGeneral.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks corroborate safe-to-store visual evidence.
settingsGeneral.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
settingsGeneral.label: Settings General tab visibly renders
settingsGeneral.proof: Computer Use clicked Settings and captured the General tab content.

settingsPreferences.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks corroborate safe-to-store visual evidence.
settingsPreferences.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
settingsPreferences.label: Settings Preferences tab visibly renders
settingsPreferences.proof: Computer Use opened Preferences and captured confirmation controls.

storybookPreview.accessibilityRiskReason: The claim has non-UI state or manifest proof in addition to UI evidence.
storybookPreview.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
storybookPreview.label: Changed UI has a direct Storybook preview when applicable
storybookPreview.limitation: Storybook preview proves inspectable isolated UI states; it does not prove native app integration by itself.
storybookPreview.proof: Changed frontend UI files are mapped to built Storybook stories and direct reviewer URLs where applicable.

suggestionCards.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks corroborate safe-to-store visual evidence.
suggestionCards.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
suggestionCards.label: Home suggestion cards are visible/clickable
suggestionCards.limitation: Does not prove every suggestion works.
suggestionCards.proof: Computer Use found and clicked a suggestion card; the UI stayed usable afterward.

summary.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks corroborate safe-to-store visual evidence.
summary.evidenceStrengthReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks provide binding visual corroboration where screenshots are safe to store.
summary.label: Summary tab renders after intent review
summary.proof: Summary text must mention the requested package/change domain.

typedIntent.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks corroborate safe-to-store visual evidence.
typedIntent.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
typedIntent.label: A typed real intent can be submitted
typedIntent.limitation: Does not prove provider execution until Review is reached.
typedIntent.proof: Prompt text appears in the Computer Use state after set_value.

updateBanner.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source without binding screenshot checks.
updateBanner.evidenceStrengthReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks provide binding visual corroboration where screenshots are safe to store.
updateBanner.label: Update banner does not block the main workflow
updateBanner.limitation: The explicit dismiss interaction is only tested on runs where a banner is actually present.
updateBanner.proof: Runner checks for a visible Dismiss button. If present, it dismisses it; if absent, it proves no update banner blocked the initial UI.

visualCoverage.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source without binding screenshot checks.
visualCoverage.evidenceStrengthReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source and screenshot signal checks provide binding visual corroboration where screenshots are safe to store.
visualCoverage.label: Core UX/UI surfaces are captured and inspectable
visualCoverage.limitation: Does not prove screenshot annotations are exact bounding boxes.
visualCoverage.proof: Required core UI surfaces have screenshot and text artifacts.

visualProofQuality.accessibilityRiskReason: The scenario is not primarily an accessibility-text assertion.
visualProofQuality.evidenceStrengthReason: Pass relies on redacted accessibility text only, usually because screenshots are intentionally suppressed for a sensitive surface.
visualProofQuality.label: Scenario results include inspectable visual/text evidence
visualProofQuality.limitation: Visual signal checks catch missing, blank, occluded, or low-signal regions; they do not prove exact design fidelity or arbitrary wrong-screen swaps.
visualProofQuality.proof: Every passing scenario must have linked proof artifacts, and required non-sensitive screenshots must pass deterministic visual signal checks.
